sections.heading: Overview
heading_path: Implementation manual WHO surgical safety checklist 2009
sections.normalized_text: As use of the Checklist has become more widespread, speciﬁc implementation factors have become more apparent. Factors for successful implementation include: Early engagement of staff Active leadership and identiﬁcation of local champions Extensive discussion, education and training Multidisciplinary involvement Coaching Ongoing feedback Local adaptation 9789241598590_eng.pdf (‎418.4Kb)‎ 9789241598590_eng_Checklist.pdf (‎92.06Kb)‎ 9789246598595_ara.pdf (‎986.2Kb)‎ 9789246598595_ara_Checklist.pdf (‎524.6Kb)‎ 9789245598596_chi.pdf (‎1.198Mb)‎ 9789245598596_chi_Checklist.pdf (‎161.9Kb)‎ 9789242598599_fre_checklist.pdf (‎485.4Kb)‎ 9789242598599_fre.pdf (‎494.0Kb)‎ 9789244598597_rus.pdf (‎522.3Kb)‎ 9789244598597_rus_Checklist.pdf (‎111.9Kb)‎ 9789243598598_spa.pdf (‎504.3Kb)‎ 9789243598598_spa_Checklist.pdf (‎489.0Kb)‎ 9789241598590_por.pdf (‎304.1Kb)‎ 9789241598590_por_Checklist.pdf (‎130.1Kb)‎ 9789241598590_jpn.pdf (‎1.339Mb)‎ 9786185250027-gre.pdf (‎4.958Mb)‎ Japanese version was published in electronic format by Niigata Prefectural Muikamachi Hospital, 636-2 Muikamachi Minami-Uonuma City, Niigata 949-6680, Japan. Portuguese version was published by the General Directorate of Health of Portugal.
